subtitles.original: ...えたのに
さよならなんだ
昔住んでた小さな部屋は
subtitles.english: The small room I used to live in

subtitles.original: 今は誰かが住んでんだ
subtitles.english: is occupied by someone else now.

subtitles.original: 君に言われたひどい言葉も
subtitles.english: The cruel words you said to me,

subtitles.original: 無駄な気がした毎日も
subtitles.english: and the days that felt meaningless too...

subtitles.original: あの時こうしていれば
subtitles.english: If only I had done things differently back then.

subtitles.original: あの日に戻れれば
subtitles.english: If only I could return to that day.

subtitles.original: あの頃の僕にはもう
subtitles.english: But to who I was in those days,

subtitles.original: 戻れないよ
subtitles.english: I can no longer go back.

subtitles.original: たとえばゆるい幸せが
subtitles.english: For example, if a gentle kind of happiness

subtitles.original: だらっと続いたとする
きっと悪い種が
芽を出して
もう
さよならなんだ
寒い冬の冷えた缶コーヒー
subtitles.english: A cold can of coffee in the freezing winter,

subtitles.original: 虹色の長いマフラー
小走りで路地裏を抜けて
subtitles.english: Jogging lightly through the back alleys,

subtitles.original: 思い出してみる
たとえばゆるい幸せが
subtitles.english: For example, if a gentle kind of happiness

subtitles.original: だらっと続いたとする
きっと悪い種が
芽を出して
もう
さよならなんだ
なんだ
なんだ
なんだ
さよなら
それもいいさ
どこかで元気でやれよ
さよなら
僕もどうにかやるさ
さよなら
そうするよ
はい！
はい、ということで！
『ソラニン』をお送りいたしました！
いかがだったでしょうか？
いや、あの...
見覚えのある方もいらっしゃるのでは
subtitles.english: I'm sure some of you might recognize this,

subtitles.original: ないでしょうか？
これ、まあ、あの、初めて見る方もいらっしゃるかなと思うんですが、
subtitles.english: Well, some of you might be seeing this for the first time,

subtitles.original: あの、FGの3Dお披露目のときにも、
subtitles.english: but back during FG's 3D debut showcase,

subtitles.original: えっと、ギターの弾き語りをお披露目させていただいたんですけれども、
subtitles.english: I performed an acoustic guitar cover then too,

subtitles.original: そのときよりもレベルアップできていたでしょうか？
subtitles.english: but do you think I've improved since then?

subtitles.original: 初めて、多分、最初の頃に練習したのが『ソラニン』で、
subtitles.english: Probably the very first song I ever practiced was "Solanin",

subtitles.original: そこからずっと、ちゃんとできた姿をみんなに見せたかったので、
subtitles.english: and since then, I've always wanted to show everyone a proper performance,

subtitles.original: この曲を選びました。
subtitles.english: so that's why I chose this song.

subtitles.original: そしてですね、ギター、ちはの音だけじゃなくて、
もう一本ソロとかベースがあったと思うんですが、
subtitles.english: there was another guitar solo and bass line, right?

subtitles.original: それはですね、ちはのギターの先生に一緒に弾いていただきました！
subtitles.english: Well, my guitar teacher actually played those parts with me!

subtitles.original: なんかレッスンのときにセッションしてたら、
subtitles.english: When we were jamming during one of our lessons,

subtitles.original: 「え、めっちゃかっこよくね？」ってなって、
「先生もよかったら一緒に弾いてくれませんか？」って言って、
subtitles.english: So I asked, "Would you like to play along with me?"

subtitles.original: 一緒にね、弾いていただきました。
subtitles.english: And they were kind enough to play together with me.

subtitles.original: ありがとうございました！

responses.thoughts:
**Subtitle Generation for "Solanin" Performance**

Okay, so I need to generate subtitles for this video segment. First, I need to meticulously analyze the audio and visual cues, aligning them with the timing. Let's start with the song "Solanin". Alright, from the very beginning of the clip, at 00:00, the singer's finishing a line that starts before the clip. The vocals at 00:00.000 are "...eta no ni," even though, followed by "Sayonara nanda," which, I realize, has to be "it's goodbye after all".

I'll sync up the lyrics with the video as precisely as I can. Now I have a good handle on the song's first verse, with the timing of the lyrics from the beginning through to the end of the song's first verse, and then the instrumental. Okay, now the second part starts at 00:26.500. Let's see... I'm hearing "Mukashi sundeta chiisana heya wa," which means "The small room I used to live in," and then the following lines: "Ima wa dareka ga sundenda," "The cruel words you said to me," and "Muda na ki ga shita mainichi mo." The timing here is key. Now, the chorus: "Ano toki kou shiteireba," "Ano hi ni modorereba," and "Ano koro no boku ni wa mou," and finally "Modorenai yo." The next part starts with "Tatoeba yurui shiawase ga," which translates as "For example, if a gentle kind of happiness had simply dragged on lazily." This is followed by "Kitto warui tane ga," and then "Me wo dashite" with a small instrumental and "Mou."

Ah, that part again at 01:28.100 is "Sayonara nanda" "it's goodbye after all." After this instrumental, let's keep working. Ok, here we are: "Samui fuyu no hieta kan koohii" for "A cold can of coffee in the freezing winter," followed by "Nijiiro no nagai mafuraa." Then there is "Kobashiri de rojiura wo nukete." I keep track, "Tatoeba yurui shiawase ga," "Daratto tsuzuitato suru," "Kitto warui tane ga," "Me wo dashite," "Mou" and back to "Sayonara nanda." It's "After all..." now, repeated three times, and then, "Sayonara," and "Sore mo ii sa," and then "Dokoka de genki de yare yo." Finally, "Goodbye... I'll manage somehow too," and then "Yeah, I will."

Alright, so the song is complete. Now the applause starts, and then there is a brief silence, followed by a sigh ("Phew!") and then a "Hai!", and the phrase "Hai, to iu koto de!". Here we go, "I just performed 'Solanin'!" and then I ask "How was it?" Then I pause, starting with a humble: "Well, you see...". Ah yes, and I realize: "I'm sure some of you might recognize this, right?". Then I go on. Okay, so I know, well, some might be seeing this for the first time... and I reminisce that this, "back during FG's 3D debut showcase, I performed an acoustic guitar cover then too." I continue, and say "But do you think I've improved since then?" I then reveal that "Probably the very first song I ever practiced was 'Solanin'" I wanted to show everyone a proper performance of it. That is why I chose this song. I talk about how the guitar isn't just my guitar's tone. I remember that the teacher played those parts, and then there was a session. "Whoa, this sounds awesome!" We jammed! Then, I then said "Would you like to play along with me?" and I said, And they were kind enough to play together with me." Here comes my gratitude. "Thank you so much!" We are finishing the segment with "With that said, let's take a look back at what we've done so far! First off, the first song of the guest segment was..." I have all the timestamps right on the money. Perfect. Now, I should have an excellent result!
